titleStmt.title: SII 30.209: original edition by N. Marxia Gandhi, A. Padmavathy and S. Swaminathan

No. 209. (A.R. No. 285 of 1915.) Vikravāṇḍi, Villuppuram Taluk, South Arcot District. Bhuvanēśvara temple-on the north, west and south walls of the central shrine Kulōttuṅga-Chōḷa I: year 32: 1102 A.D.

summary.p: Fragment. This inscription records the gift for a perpetual lamp to the temple of Chēdikulachintāmaṇi-Īśvaramuḍaiyār at Gaṅgaikoṉḍach-chōḻapuram by a merchant of this village named Sēntanūrāḻvaṉ Moḍanallūr Nakkaṉ alias Aṟivaikaḷ Kaṟpaka Vēḷāṉ.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svastiśrī . pukaḻmātu viḷaṅka jayamātu virumpa nilamakaḷ nilava malar]makaḷ puṇara Urimaiyiṟ ciṟanta maṇi muṭicūṭi miṉavar nilaikeṭa villavar kulaitara Eṉaimaṉṉa
naṭātti virasimhāsaṉattu Avaṉimuḻutuṭaiyāḷoṭu viṟṟirun=taruḷiya kovirājakecaripanmarā ṅka coḻa devaṟkku yāṇṭu 3 10 2 ṉāl kaṅkaikoṇṭacoḻapu
rattuc cetikulacin=tāmaṇi Īśvaramuṭaiyārkku Ivvūr viyāpāri cen=ta nūrāḻvaāṉ moṭanallūr nakkaṉāṉa Aṟivaikaḷ kaṟpakaveḷāṉ Iṉnakarattār pakkal koṇṭu Ārkkaṭṭukki�
Oru tirunan=tāviḷakku muṭṭāme Erikkakaṭavomisthānattu śivabrāhmaṇa rom Itu cantrādittavaṟ śrīpanmāheśvararakṣai ..
* Two lines of a fragmentary inscription of the 10th century characters are incised above this inscription, which reads:

rkkaṇakkaṉ ceykiṟa pekaiyūruṭaiyāṉ
māruñ ceykiṟa śrīrājarājac catuvetima
Digital edition of SII 30.209 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
